description: Rational number arithmetic. Provides the Fraction class for exact representation of rational numbers and arithmetic operations.

functions.name: gcd

returns.description: The greatest common divisor of a and b

functions.description: Returns the greatest common divisor of the integers a and b. If either a or b is nonzero, the value of gcd(a, b) is the largest positive integer that divides both a and b. gcd(0, 0) returns 0.

classes.name: Fraction
classes.description: Represents a rational number as a pair of integers (numerator and denominator). Fraction instances are immutable and hashable, and can be used anywhere a rational number is expected. All standard arithmetic operations are supported with exact results.

params.name: numerator
params.description: The numerator of the fraction. Default is 0.

params.name: denominator
params.description: The denominator of the fraction. Must be nonzero. Default is 1.

constructors.description: Creates a Fraction from an integer numerator and denominator. The fraction is automatically normalized: the sign is always carried by the numerator, and the GCD is divided out.

params.name: float
params.description: A floating-point number to convert to an exact Fraction representation

constructors.description: Creates a Fraction from a float. The result exactly equals the float value. For example, Fraction(0.5) gives Fraction(1, 2), but Fraction(0.1) gives Fraction(3602879701896397, 36028797018963968) due to float imprecision.

params.name: decimal
params.description: A decimal.Decimal instance to convert to a Fraction

constructors.description: Creates a Fraction from a Decimal instance. The result exactly equals the Decimal value.

params.name: string
params.description: A string in the form 'numerator/denominator' (e.g. '3/7'), a decimal string (e.g. '0.25', '.5', '1.'), or a string with optional sign (e.g. '-3/7')

constructors.description: Creates a Fraction from a string. Accepted forms: '[-]numerator[/denominator]' (e.g. '3/7', '-1/3'), decimal notation (e.g. '0.25', '1.'), and strings with leading/trailing whitespace.

methods.name: limit_denominator

returns.description: The closest rational number with denominator at most max_denominator

methods.description: Finds the closest Fraction to self that has denominator at most max_denominator. Useful for finding rational approximations to floats: Fraction(3.141592653589793).limit_denominator(100) gives Fraction(311, 99).

methods.name: __add__

returns.description: The sum as a Fraction (exact when adding with int or Fraction)

methods.description: Returns self + other. When adding two Fractions or a Fraction and an int, the result is exact. When adding with a float, the float is first converted to a Fraction.

methods.name: __sub__
methods.description: Returns self - other. Supports Fraction, int, and float operands.

methods.name: __mul__
methods.description: Returns self * other. Supports Fraction, int, and float operands.

methods.name: __truediv__
methods.description: Returns self / other. In Python 2.7, this is invoked by the / operator when from __future__ import division is active, or by calling Fraction.__truediv__ directly. Raises ZeroDivisionError if other is zero.

fields.name: numerator
fields.description: The numerator of the fraction in lowest terms

fields.name: denominator
fields.description: The denominator of the fraction in lowest terms (always positive)
